ip_outcome_note: Card name is 'Frieza // Bionic Strike Mecha Frieza'. Both sides of the flip card are named after Frieza. The card art (per Vision section) shows Frieza's distinctive pale-skinned alien Frieza-clan face — no ambiguity. Frieza is a Bandai/Toei Animation-owned character, not a Wizards-internal character. ip_verified set to true, ip_confidence raised to high. External IP callout block warranted.

trivia_bullets.text: P-028 is a DBS Card Game Promotion Card — a distributed promo, not part of a standard booster set. The card is a Leader flip card: the base side is 'Frieza' and the awakened side is 'Bionic Strike Mecha Frieza.' Mecha Frieza is the mechanically-rebuilt form of Frieza following his death at the hands of Future Trunks in the Dragon Ball Z Trunks Saga. [Verified IP: Bandai/Toei Animation]

P-028 was added to the official DBS Card Game Watch List due to its role in enabling turn-two kill strategies. The Bionic Strike Mecha Frieza awakened side reduced Extra Card costs while generating card advantage via life trading, enabling powerful plays like BT3-003 Victorious Fist Super Saiyan 3 Son Goku by turn two. Bandai subsequently issued errata to P-028's [Permanent] skill. [WebSearch: dbs-cardgame.com/us-en/strategy/errata/p028-frieza.php]
sources: WebSearch: DBS Card Game official errata page for P-028

trivia_bullets.text: As a promo Leader card, P-028 was distributed through event or store channels rather than booster packs — the image resolution in inventory (260x363, low quality) reflects the source image from dbs-cardgame.com's promotional image hosting, not a full-resolution scan. [Scryfall-equivalent: dbs-cardgame.com card image]
